intro: Oxydectes gracilis, also known as 'Milk Vetch', is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the Fabaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including respiratory issues and digestive problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Oxydectes gracilis include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal effects. In traditional herbal medicine, Oxydectes gracilis is often prepared into teas, infusions, and tinctures to address a range of health concerns, including coughs, colds, and skin issues.
